claims.claim: add_word_timestamps/_add_word_timestamps stamp each word with PTS, push word-level TTSTextFrames, track _word_last_pts
claims.found: L1185 'async def add_word_timestamps'; L1233 'async def _add_word_timestamps'; L1257 'pts = self._initial_word_timestamp + ts_ns'; L1264 'self._word_last_pts = f.pts' pushing TTSTextFrame

claims.claim: Cartesia returns word_timestamps:{words,start,end} on socket, normalized then fed to add_word_timestamps(..., sample_rate, context_id)
claims.found: L680-682 reads only msg['word_timestamps']['words'] and ['start'] (no 'end'); L683-689 calls add_word_timestamps(processed_timestamps, ctx_id, includes_inter_frame_spaces=...). No sample_rate is passed and add_word_timestamps has no sample_rate parameter (sig: word_times, context_id, includes_inter_frame_spaces, pre_merge_tokens).
claims.correction: Cartesia code consumes only 'words' and 'start'; remove the 'end' field and the 'sample_rate' argument. Call signature: add_word_timestamps(processed_timestamps, ctx_id, includes_inter_frame_spaces=...).

claims.claim: ElevenLabs returns char alignment (chars, charStartTimesMs) which calculate_word_times folds into word timestamps, carrying partial word across chunk boundaries
claims.found: L335 'def calculate_word_times'; L355-356 chars = alignment_info['chars']; char_start_times_ms = alignment_info['charStartTimesMs']; L367 'current_word = partial_word  # Start with any partial word from previous chunk'

claims.claim: prefers normalized_alignment only when a pronunciation dictionary is active to avoid duplicated words
claims.found: L262 'def _select_alignment'; L279-281 'Prefer normalized only when a pronunciation dictionary is configured - that's the case where alignment has overlapping restarts that produce duplicated/garbled words'; L295 'if prefer_normalized: return msg.get(normalized_key) or ...'

claims.claim: assistant aggregation frame stamped pts = _word_last_pts + 1 so it flushes after every spoken word frame
claims.found: L819-820 'if self._word_last_pts: aggregation_frame.pts = self._word_last_pts + 1'; L811 builds LLMAssistantPushAggregationFrame; comment L816-818 'forces it through the clock queue too, so the assistant aggregator sees every word before flushing'

claims.found: L564 'AgentStartedSpeaking, and AgentAudioDone'; L557 'events such as AgentAudioDone provide a clean boundary for recovery'

claims.claim: LiveKit carries aligned transcript via push_timed_transcript/TimedString in StreamAdapter
Frontmatter code_refs resolve to the cited symbols
claims.found: tts_service.py:1233 = '_add_word_timestamps'; elevenlabs/tts.py:336 inside calculate_word_times (def at 335); cartesia/tts.py:680 = _normalize_word_timestamps call in timestamps branch

summary: 8/9 supported. One WRONG: the Cartesia paragraph overstates the wire format ('end' field) and misstates the call signature ('sample_rate' arg) — the code reads only words+start and add_word_timestamps takes no sample_rate.
